lines.en_lit: but to preserve a life, even from on top of My altar, Just as this there is uncertainty whether there is substance to his words or whether there is no substance to his words, and service overrides Shabbat, a fortiori, saving a life overrides Shabbat. Rabbi Elazar answered and said: Just as of circumcision, which one of the 248 limbs of the body, overrides Shabbat, a fortiori, one’s whole body, overrides Shabbat.

Rabbi Yosei, son of Rabbi Yehuda, says it keep my Shabbatot” might to everyone the verse states “but,” qualifies. Rabbi Yonatan ben Yosef says it “For it is sacred to you” is given into your hands, and you are not given to it

Rabbi Shimon ben Menasya said: “And the children of Israel shall keep Shabbat, The Torah said: Desecrate one Shabbat on his behalf so he will observe many Shabbatot. Rav Yehuda said Shmuel said: If I would there I would have said my proof is preferable to theirs, and live by them” and not that he die by them.

Rava All of these have refutations except for of Shmuel, which has no refutation. by Rabbi Yishmael perhaps of Rava, Rava said: What is the reason about breaks in? a presumption a person does not restrain himself with respect to his money. And this knows that will rise to oppose him said If he rises against me, I will kill him. And the Torah states: comes to kill you, rise to kill him first. We found certain from where do we uncertainty
of Rabbi Akiva also perhaps in accordance with of Abaye, as Abaye said: we send a pair of rabbis his to determine if his words substance. we found certain from where do we uncertain?

And for all we found certain from where do we that Shmuel there is certainly no refutation. Ravina said, and some say it was Rav Naḥman bar Yitzḥak One spicy pepper is better than a whole basket of squash,

MISHNA: A sin-offering, a definite guilt-offering, atone Death and Yom Kippur atone by repentance. Repentance atones for minor transgressions, for positive and negative And for severe in abeyance until Yom Kippur comes and atones one who says: I will sin and I will repent, I will sin and I will repent, does not provide him the opportunity to repent, I will sin and Yom Kippur atone Yom Kippur does not atone transgressions between a person and God, Yom Kippur atones; transgressions between a person and another, Yom Kippur does not atone until he appeases other

Rabbi Elazar ben Azarya taught “From all your sins you shall be cleansed before the Lord” transgressions between a person and God, Yom Kippur atones; transgressions between a person and another, Yom Kippur does not atone until he appeases other Rabbi Akiva said: How fortunate are you, Israel; before Whom are you purified, and Who purifies you? your Father in Heaven, as it is stated: “And I will sprinkle purifying water upon you, and you shall be purified” And it says: “The ritual bath of Israel is God” Just as a ritual bath purifies the impure, so too, the Holy One, Blessed be He, purifies Israel.

GEMARA: a definite guilt-offering, yes, an uncertain guilt-offering not. But isn’t atonement written with regard to it? Those, facilitate complete atonement, an uncertain guilt-offering does not facilitate complete atonement.

Alternatively, Nothing else facilitates the atonement of those else does facilitate the atonement of the uncertain guilt-offering, as we learned Yom Kippur passed, those who are obligated to bring sin-offerings and definite guilt-offerings are obligated uncertain guilt-offerings are exempt

death and Yom Kippur atone by repentance. With repentance, yes, but by themselves, they do not. Let us say not in accordance with Rabbi as it was taught Rabbi says for all transgressions in the Torah, whether one repented or did not repent, Yom Kippur atones, exception rejecting the yoke and interpreting the Torah falsely; and violating the covenant of the flesh, if one repents Yom Kippur atones and if one does not repent Yom Kippur does not atone
even you say Rabbi Repentance requires Yom Kippur Yom Kippur does not require repentance

Repentance atones for minor transgressions, for a positive and for a negative Now atones for a negative necessary for a positive Rav Yehuda said: This is it said, for a positive and for a negative can be rectified by a positive
But not for a full-fledged negative raises a contradiction these are minor A positive and a negative
